frameworks.name: Category Adjustment Model (CAM)
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian account where memory reconstruction biases stimuli toward category center.

frameworks.name: generalized CAM (g-CAM)
frameworks.one_line: Proposed Bayesian extension unifying typical and atypical category memory effects.

frameworks.name: Bayesian reconstructive memory
frameworks.one_line: Prior knowledge combined with noisy memory trace at multiple abstraction levels.

core_question: Can a generalized Bayesian model unify reconstructive memory effects for both typical and atypical category exemplars?
key_assumptions: Memory reconstruction integrates prior knowledge with noisy traces
Prior knowledge operates at multiple levels of abstraction
Category-level prior pulls remembered stimulus toward category center
manipulations: Typicality of category exemplars (typical vs atypical)
Comparison of model variants (CAM vs g-CAM) via simulation
key_findings: Atypical exemplars show weaker pull toward category center than typical exemplars
g-CAM reproduces previously known CAM effects in simulation
g-CAM additionally captures atypicality-dependent memory bias
interpretations: A generalized Bayesian framework unifies typical/atypical reconstructive memory under one model
Reconstructive memory effects depend on exemplar typicality within category structure
